positional_bias.note: Re-derived = index per MCQ from quiz.gift; matches sidecar correct_position_distribution exactly. All 4 positions used; max_share 0.333 <= 0.40.

length_bias.note: Measured every option char length (feedback stripped). No correct (=) option exceeds 1.20x mean of its distractors. Re-derived longest-correct count = 0/6 (stricter than meta's 1/6); both well below 0.40.

distractor_plausibility.note: All 18 distractors map to real beginner misconceptions (server break-in/password theft, DoS, source-code rewrite, admin-role trust, timer-forgetting, typing speed, images-vs-text, free-vs-paid, same-attack-conflation). q5's non-harm/irrelevant-defence pairs are the weakest (score ~2) but coherent 'what counts as harm' confusions, not absurd. No distractor scored 1.

objective_coverage.note: All 6 corpus section-3 LOs each have exactly one matching question (q1-q6 1:1). No orphans.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: Plain-language stems, no undefined acronyms (LLM not relied on in stems). Tests prompt-injection only; no governance-framework or exploit-craft trivia. q5 defences are beginner-level from corpus 8. Jailbreak appears only in q6 as taught (corpus 5.6) disambiguation.

gift_validity.note: Clean parse. $CATEGORY present. Each block ::qN:: stem { one = + three ~ }, every answer line carries well-formed #feedback. #### general feedback well-formed. No stem-level #. Colons escaped where needed (q5 'Impact\:' / 'defence\:').

verdict: Pass - all bias / coverage / scope / GIFT checks clean. Positional max_share 0.333, longest-correct 0/6, absurd_rate 0.0, all 6 LOs covered 1:1, on-headline and beginner-fair, GIFT valid. Sidecar matches gift (no desync).
